pr.title: Match gmadventure, link, and ninja apply
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched src/melee/gm/gmadventure.c by factoring a repeated gm_8016A22C setup sequence into a shared helper, updating local sdata2 symbols to compiler-style @ labels, and flipping gmadventure.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev report credited one new match, gm_801B4974, moving main/melee/gm/gmadventure to 100% for that item and increasing matched code by 436 bytes.
what_changed: Added static helper gm_8016A22C_inline(MinorScene* arg0, UnkAdventureData* temp_r29) in gmadventure.c.
Moved the repeated gm_8017E48C, gm_8017E4C4, gm_8016A22C argument setup, and x0.xC.x11 assignment into gm_8016A22C_inline.
Replaced an inlined gm_8016A22C call sequence in gm_801B4974 with gm_8016A22C_inline(arg0, temp_r29).
Replaced the same repeated call sequence inside inline0 with gm_8016A22C_inline(scene, adv_data).
Removed now-unneeded local variable temp_r25_2 from gm_801B4974.
Changed configure.py entry for melee/gm/gmadventure.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Renamed six local .sdata2 symbols at 0x804DAC18 through 0x804DAC2C from gm_804DACxx-style generated names to local @217, @218, @244, @306, @307, and @330 labels.
smart_moves: Recognized that two similar long gm_8016A22C setup blocks should be represented as a shared helper, likely matching the original compiler's inline/static helper structure better than duplicated hand-written code.
Kept the helper narrow: it only computes the adventure count, retrieves gm_803DE650_t from the MinorScene index, performs the gm_8016A22C call, and marks x0.xC.x11 as initialized.

Preserved the apparently order-sensitive dummy calls in inline0 to gm_80169520 and gm_80169540 while only replacing the repeated setup block, reducing risk to matching-sensitive scheduling/register allocation.
Updated configure.py immediately after achieving the object match so build metadata reflects that gmadventure.c is now a matching object.
Converted unnamed/local constants in symbols.txt back to @-style local labels, avoiding misleading global-looking gm_804DACxx names for local .sdata2 data.
decomp_lessons: When a nonmatching function contains a long call setup also present elsewhere, test whether the original source used a shared inline/static helper; factoring it can improve register allocation and instruction ordering.
For matching work, duplicated source can be less accurate than a small helper if the compiler emitted the same sequence at multiple call sites.

Do not delete unused-looking calls blindly in nonmatching code: calls like gm_80169520 and gm_80169540 remained in inline0 even though their results are assigned to unused temporaries, suggesting they were needed for codegen or side effects.
After a file becomes fully matching, remember to change its configure.py Object state from NonMatching to Matching.
Local .sdata2 constants should use local @ labels when evidence supports them; address-derived gm_804Dxxxx names can be placeholders rather than stable symbol names.
naming_conventions: The new helper was named gm_8016A22C_inline, tying it to the callee sequence it wraps and marking it as an inline-style matching artifact.
Local .sdata2 entries were represented with @NNN labels rather than gm_804DACxx names when they are local compiler constants.
Existing temporary-style names such as temp_r29, temp_r30, temp_r24_2, and temp_r31 were retained; this PR did not attempt broader semantic renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Factored the gm_8016A22C argument construction into a static helper to alter inlining/codegen shape for gm_801B4974 and inline0.
Removed a local variable from gm_801B4974 after its work moved into the helper, changing register pressure at the caller.
Kept helper parameters as MinorScene* and UnkAdventureData*, allowing the helper to recompute count and gm_803DE650_t internally rather than passing many precomputed values.
Adjusted local data labels in symbols.txt to match expected local constant references.
Marked gmadventure.c Matching only after the decomp.dev report showed a new match for gm_801B4974.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 reported matched code 55.14% (+0.01%, +436 bytes), linked code 31.75% (+0.13%, +5092 bytes), linked data 29.16% (+0.16%, +1996 bytes).
Automated report listed one new match: main/melee/gm/gmadventure item gm_801B4974, +5 bytes, from 99.04% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Look for other gmadventure functions with repeated gm_8016A22C setup that may also want gm_8016A22C_inline or a similar helper.
Audit remaining gm_804Dxxxx local .sdata2 placeholder names near gmadventure for possible conversion to @ local labels when matching evidence appears.
Search for unused temporary calls retained for matching, such as gm_80169520 and gm_80169540, before removing them in cleanup PRs.
